output:
# solution.py
"""Team Collaboration Manager.

A self-contained, thread-safe, in-memory collaboration backend that powers
the kind of multi-user workflow described by the ArtCollab brief: users,
projects, tasks, threaded messaging with attachments, a performance
dashboard, and exportable reports (CSV / PDF).

The implementation deliberately uses only the Python standard library so it
can run anywhere without external services. State lives in memory behind a
single shared re-entrant lock so the manager and all of its sub-stores are
safe to drive from many concurrent clients (the WebSocket fan-out scenario).

Design highlights
-----------------
* One global ``RLock`` shared by the manager and every sub-store, giving
  consistent, dead-lock-free coordination across cross-store operations.
* A single monotonic id generator so identifiers are globally unique across
  every entity type.
* Getter isolation: every public accessor returns deep copies, so callers can
  never mutate stored state by accident.
* Secondary indexes (by project, by assignee, by task, by ratee) keep lookups
  O(1)/O(k) instead of scanning the whole dataset under load.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Set


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle states for a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class UserRole(Enum):
    """Authorization role assigned to a user."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Discriminator stored on every generated report."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for all errors raised by the manager."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an action."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint is violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """A file attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime


@dataclass
class User:
    """A registered user account."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A project with an owner and a date window."""

    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work within a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus
    assignee_id: Optional[int]
    deadline: Optional[datetime]
    started_at: Optional[datetime]
    completed_at: Optional[datetime]
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A message posted to a project or task, optionally threaded."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int]
    task_id: Optional[int]
    parent_id: Optional[int]
    attachments: List[Attachment]
    created_at: datetime
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating from one user to another."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str
    task_id: Optional[int]
    created_at: datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _clone(obj):
    """Return a deep copy so callers cannot mutate stored state."""

    return copy.deepcopy(obj)


def _require_non_empty_string(value, message):
    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
        raise ValidationError(message)


def _validate_email(email):
    if not isinstance(email, str) or not email.strip() or "@" not in email:
        raise ValidationError("invalid email address")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Base store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _BaseStore:
    """Common base giving every store access to the manager and shared lock."""

    def __init__(self, manager, lock):
        self._manager = manager
        self._lock = lock


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# User store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserStore(_BaseStore):
    def __init__(self, manager, lock):
        super().__init__(manager, lock)
        self._users: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self._by_username: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._by_email: Dict[str, int] = {}

    # -- internal (lock assumed held, returns raw references) ---------------
    def _require(self, user_id) -> User:
        user = self._users.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def _exists(self, user_id) -> bool:
        return user_id in self._users

    # -- public API ---------------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            _require_non_empty_string(username, "username is required")
            _validate_email(email)
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            username = username.strip()
            email = email.strip()
            if username in self._by_username:
                raise DuplicateError("username already exists")
            if email in self._by_email:
                raise DuplicateError("email already exists")
            uid = self._manager._next_id()
            user = User(
                id=uid,
                username=username,
                email=email,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._manager._now(),
            )
            self._users[uid] = user
            self._by_username[username] = uid
            self._by_email[email] = uid
            return _clone(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._require(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            return [_clone(self._users[k]) for k in sorted(self._users)]

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=None, email=None, role=None) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require(user_id)
            new_username = user.username if username is None else username
            new_email = user.email if email is None else email
            new_role = user.role if role is None else role

            _require_non_empty_string(new_username, "username is required")
            _validate_email(new_email)
            if not isinstance(new_role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            new_username = new_username.strip()
            new_email = new_email.strip()

            existing_u = self._by_username.get(new_username)
            if existing_u is not None and existing_u != user_id:
                raise DuplicateError("username already exists")
            existing_e = self._by_email.get(new_email)
            if existing_e is not None and existing_e != user_id:
                raise DuplicateError("email already exists")

            if new_username != user.username:
                del self._by_username[user.username]
                self._by_username[new_username] = user_id
                user.username = new_username
            if new_email != user.email:
                del self._by_email[user.email]
                self._by_email[new_email] = user_id
                user.email = new_email
            user.role = new_role
            return _clone(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require(user_id)
            if self._manager.projects._owned_by(user_id):
                raise ConflictError("user owns one or more projects")
            if self._manager.tasks._has_open_task(user_id):
                raise ConflictError("user has open assigned tasks")
            del self._by_username[user.username]
            del self._by_email[user.email]
            del self._users[user_id]
            # Detach any remaining (completed) tasks from the deleted user.
            self._manager.tasks._drop_assignee(user_id)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class ProjectStore(_BaseStore):
    def __init__(self, manager, lock):
        super().__init__(manager, lock)
        self._projects: Dict[int, Project] = {}

    # -- internal -----------------------------------------------------------
    def _require(self, project_id) -> Project:
        project = self._projects.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def _owned_by(self, user_id) -> bool:
        return any(p.owner_id == user_id for p in self._projects.values())

    # -- public API ---------------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(
        self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description=""
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.users._require(owner_id)
            _require_non_empty_string(name, "project name is required")
            if start_date is None or end_date is None:
                raise ValidationError("start_date and end_date are required")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot precede start_date")
            pid = self._manager._next_id()
            project = Project(
                id=pid,
                name=name.strip(),
                description=description or "",
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                created_at=self._manager._now(),
            )
            self._projects[pid] = project
            return _clone(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._require(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            return [_clone(self._projects[k]) for k in sorted(self._projects)]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id,
        name=None,
        description=None,
        start_date=None,
        end_date=None,
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require(project_id)
            new_name = project.name if name is None else name
            new_desc = project.description if description is None else description
            new_start = project.start_date if start_date is None else start_date
            new_end = project.end_date if end_date is None else end_date

            _require_non_empty_string(new_name, "project name is required")
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot precede start_date")

            project.name = new_name.strip()
            project.description = new_desc or ""
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            return _clone(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._require(project_id)
            self._manager.tasks._delete_by_project(project_id)
            self._manager.messages._delete_by_project(project_id)
            del self._projects[project_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Task store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStore(_BaseStore):
    def __init__(self, manager, lock):
        super().__init__(manager, lock)
        self._tasks: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self._by_project: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}
        self._by_assignee: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}

    # -- internal (lock assumed held, raw references) -----------------------
    def _require(self, task_id) -> Task:
        task = self._tasks.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def _all_tasks(self) -> List[Task]:
        return list(self._tasks.values())

    def _tasks_for_project(self, project_id) -> List[Task]:
        return [self._tasks[t] for t in self._by_project.get(project_id, ())]

    def _tasks_for_assignee(self, user_id) -> List[Task]:
        return [self._tasks[t] for t in self._by_assignee.get(user_id, ())]

    def _has_open_task(self, user_id) -> bool:
        for tid in self._by_assignee.get(user_id, ()):
            if self._tasks[tid].status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                return True
        return False

    def _drop_assignee(self, user_id) -> None:
        tids = self._by_assignee.pop(user_id, set())
        for tid in tids:
            task = self._tasks.get(tid)
            if task is not None:
                task.assignee_id = None

    def _delete_by_project(self, project_id) -> None:
        tids = list(self._by_project.get(project_id, ()))
        for tid in tids:
            task = self._tasks.pop(tid, None)
            if task is not None and task.assignee_id is not None:
                self._by_assignee.get(task.assignee_id, set()).discard(tid)
        self._by_project.pop(project_id, None)

    def _add_to_assignee_index(self, assignee_id, task_id) -> None:
        self._by_assignee.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(task_id)

    def _remove_from_assignee_index(self, assignee_id, task_id) -> None:
        bucket = self._by_assignee.get(assignee_id)
        if bucket is not None:
            bucket.discard(task_id)

    # -- public API ---------------------------------------------------------
    def create_task(self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._manager.projects._require(project_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._manager.users._require(assignee_id)
            _require_non_empty_string(title, "task title is required")
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline cannot precede project start")
            tid = self._manager._next_id()
            task = Task(
                id=tid,
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title.strip(),
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                started_at=None,
                completed_at=None,
                created_at=self._manager._now(),
            )
            self._tasks[tid] = task
            self._by_project.setdefault(project_id, set()).add(tid)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._add_to_assignee_index(assignee_id, tid)
            return _clone(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._require(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.projects._require(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self._by_project.get(project_id, ()))
            return [_clone(self._tasks[i]) for i in ids]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.users._require(assignee_id)
            ids = sorted(self._by_assignee.get(assignee_id, ()))
            return [_clone(self._tasks[i]) for i in ids]

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require(task_id)
            self._manager.users._require(assignee_id)
            if task.assignee_id is not None:
                self._remove_from_assignee_index(task.assignee_id, task_id)
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            self._add_to_assignee_index(assignee_id, task_id)
            return _clone(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, new_assignee_id) -> Task:
        # Re-assignment shares the same semantics as assignment.
        return self.assign_task(task_id, new_assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require(task_id)
            if task.assignee_id is not None:
                self._remove_from_assignee_index(task.assignee_id, task_id)
                task.assignee_id = None
            return _clone(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._manager.projects._require(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline cannot precede project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return _clone(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            now = self._manager._now()
            if status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            elif status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            task.status = status
            return _clone(task)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Message store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class MessageStore(_BaseStore):
    def __init__(self, manager, lock):
        super().__init__(manager, lock)
        self._messages: Dict[int, Message] = {}
        self._by_project: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}
        self._by_task: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}

    # -- internal -----------------------------------------------------------
    def _require(self, message_id) -> Message:
        message = self._messages.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    def _build_attachments(self, attachments, author_id) -> List[Attachment]:
        result: List[Attachment] = []
        if not attachments:
            return result
        now = self._manager._now()
        for item in attachments:
            filename, data = item
            if not isinstance(filename, str) or not filename:
                raise ValidationError("attachment filename is required")
            size = len(data) if data is not None else 0
            result.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=size,
                    uploaded_by=author_id,
                    uploaded_at=now,
                )
            )
        return result

    def _delete_by_project(self, project_id) -> None:
        mids = list(self._by_project.get(project_id, ()))
        for mid in mids:
            message = self._messages.pop(mid, None)
            if message is not None and message.task_id is not None:
                self._by_task.get(message.task_id, set()).discard(mid)
        self._by_project.pop(project_id, None)

    # -- public API ---------------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id=None,
        task_id=None,
        parent_id=None,
        attachments=None,
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.users._require(author_id)
            if project_id is None and task_id is None:
                raise ValidationError("a project_id or task_id is required")
            if project_id is not None and task_id is not None:
                raise ValidationError("provide only one of project_id or task_id")
            if project_id is not None:
                self._manager.projects._require(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._manager.tasks._require(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require(parent_id)

            built_attachments = self._build_attachments(attachments, author_id)
            body = body if isinstance(body, str) else ""
            if not body.strip() and not built_attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message must have a body or attachment")

            mid = self._manager._next_id()
            message = Message(
                id=mid,
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=built_attachments,
                created_at=self._manager._now(),
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self._messages[mid] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                self._by_project.setdefault(project_id, set()).add(mid)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._by_task.setdefault(task_id, set()).add(mid)
            return _clone(message)

    def edit_message(self, message_id, author_id, body) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require(message_id)
            if message.author_id != author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit this message")
            _require_non_empty_string(body, "message body is required")
            message.body = body
            message.edited_at = self._manager._now()
            return _clone(message)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, author_id, filename, data) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require(message_id)
            if message.author_id != author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may attach files")
            if not isinstance(filename, str) or not filename:
                raise ValidationError("attachment filename is required")
            attachment = Attachment(
                filename=filename,
                size=len(data) if data is not None else 0,
                uploaded_by=author_id,
                uploaded_at=self._manager._now(),
            )
            message.attachments.append(attachment)
            return _clone(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.projects._require(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self._by_project.get(project_id, ()))
            return [_clone(self._messages[i]) for i in ids]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.tasks._require(task_id)
            ids = sorted(self._by_task.get(task_id, ()))
            return [_clone(self._messages[i]) for i in ids]

    def delete_message(self, message_id, author_id) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require(message_id)
            if message.author_id != author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete this message")
            if message.project_id is not None:
                self._by_project.get(message.project_id, set()).discard(message_id)
            if message.task_id is not None:
                self._by_task.get(message.task_id, set()).discard(message_id)
            del self._messages[message_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Performance store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class PerformanceStore(_BaseStore):
    def __init__(self, manager, lock):
        super().__init__(manager, lock)
        self._ratings: Dict[int, Rating] = {}
        self._by_ratee: Dict[int, Set[int]] = {}

    # -- public API ---------------------------------------------------------
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.users._require(user_id)
            tasks = self._manager.tasks._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.users._require(user_id)
            tasks = self._manager.tasks._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            durations = []
            for task in tasks:
                if (
                    task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    and task.started_at is not None
                    and task.completed_at is not None
                ):
                    durations.append(
                        (task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds()
                    )
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def submit_rating(
        self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="", task_id=None
    ) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("users cannot rate themselves")
            self._manager.users._require(rater_id)
            self._manager.users._require(ratee_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._manager.tasks._require(task_id)
            if (
                not isinstance(score, int)
                or isinstance(score, bool)
                or score < 1
                or score > 5
            ):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer between 1 and 5")
            rid = self._manager._next_id()
            rating = Rating(
                id=rid,
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._manager._now(),
            )
            self._ratings[rid] = rating
            self._by_ratee.setdefault(ratee_id, set()).add(rid)
            return _clone(rating)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.users._require(user_id)
            rids = sorted(self._by_ratee.get(user_id, ()))
            ratings = [_clone(self._ratings[r]) for r in rids]
            count = len(ratings)
            average = sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            return {"count": count, "average": average, "ratings": ratings}

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.users._require(user_id)
            tasks = self._manager.tasks._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0
            average_time = self.get_average_completion_time(user_id)
            summary = self.get_ratings_summary(user_id)
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "average_completion_time": average_time,
                "rating_count": summary["count"],
                "rating_average": summary["average"],
            }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Report store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class ReportStore(_BaseStore):
    # -- report builders ----------------------------------------------------
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.projects._require(project_id)
            tasks = self._manager.tasks._tasks_for_project(project_id)
            now = self._manager._now()
            total = len(tasks)
            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            completed = 0
            overdue = 0
            per_assignee: Dict[int, dict] = {}
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                if (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    and now > task.deadline
                ):
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                        task.assignee_id, {"total": 0, "completed": 0}
                    )
                    bucket["total"] += 1
                    if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        bucket["completed"] += 1
            completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._manager.projects._require(project_id)
                source_tasks = self._manager.tasks._tasks_for_project(project_id)
            else:
                source_tasks = self._manager.tasks._all_tasks()

            user_ids = {
                t.assignee_id for t in source_tasks if t.assignee_id is not None
            }
            rows = []
            for uid in sorted(user_ids):
                tasks = self._manager.tasks._tasks_for_assignee(uid)
                if project_id is not None:
                    tasks = [t for t in tasks if t.project_id == project_id]
                total = len(tasks)
                completed = sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                )
                in_progress = sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
                )
                rows.append(
                    {
                        "user_id": uid,
                        "total_tasks": total,
                        "completed_tasks": completed,
                        "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                        "completion_rate": completed / total if total else 0.0,
                    }
                )
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._manager.users._require(user_id)
            tasks = self._manager.tasks._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            task_rows = []
            for task in sorted(tasks, key=lambda t: t.id):
                task_rows.append(
                    {
                        "task_id": task.id,
                        "title": task.title,
                        "status": task.status.value,
                        "project_id": task.project_id,
                    }
                )
            average_time = self._manager.performance.get_average_completion_time(
                user_id
            )
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "average_completion_time": average_time,
                "tasks": task_rows,
            }

    # -- export -------------------------------------------------------------
    def export_report(self, report, report_format):
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(report, dict):
                raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")
            if not isinstance(report_format, ReportFormat):
                raise ValidationError("report_format must be a ReportFormat")
            if report_format == ReportFormat.CSV:
                return self._to_csv(report)
            return self._to_pdf(report)

    @staticmethod
    def _csv_cell(value):
        if isinstance(value, (dict, list)):
            return repr(value)
        if value is None:
            return ""
        return value

    def _to_csv(self, report) -> str:
        output = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(output)
        rows = report.get("rows")
        if isinstance(rows, list):
            keys: List[str] = []
            for row in rows:
                if isinstance(row, dict):
                    for key in row.keys():
                        if key not in keys:
                            keys.append(key)
            if not keys:
                keys = ["report_type"]
                writer.writerow(keys)
                writer.writerow([report.get("report_type", "")])
                return output.getvalue()
            writer.writerow(keys)
            for row in rows:
                writer.writerow([self._csv_cell(row.get(key)) for key in keys])
        else:
            writer.writerow(["field", "value"])
            for key, value in report.items():
                writer.writerow([key, self._csv_cell(value)])
        return output.getvalue()

    def _to_pdf(self, report) -> bytes:
        return _build_pdf(self._report_to_lines(report))

    @staticmethod
    def _report_to_lines(report) -> List[str]:
        lines = ["Report: {0}".format(report.get("report_type", "report"))]
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            if isinstance(value, list):
                lines.append("{0}: {1} item(s)".format(key, len(value)))
            elif isinstance(value, dict):
                lines.append("{0}:".format(key))
                for sub_key, sub_value in value.items():
                    lines.append("  {0}: {1}".format(sub_key, sub_value))
            else:
                lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, value))
        return lines


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimal PDF generation (standard library only)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _build_pdf(lines: List[str]) -> bytes:
    """Build a small but structurally valid single-page PDF document."""

    def escape(text):
        return (
            str(text)
            .replace("\\", "\\\\")
            .replace("(", "\\(")
            .replace(")", "\\)")
        )

    if not lines:
        lines = [""]

    content_parts = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "50 750 Td", "14 TL"]
    content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(escape(lines[0])))
    for line in lines[1:]:
        content_parts.append("T*")
        content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(escape(line)))
    content_parts.append("ET")
    content = "\n".join(content_parts).encode("latin-1", "replace")

    objects = [
        b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
        b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
        (
            b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
            b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>"
        ),
        b"<< /Length "
        + str(len(content)).encode("ascii")
        + b" >>\nstream\n"
        + content
        + b"\nendstream",
        b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
    ]

    pdf = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n")
    offsets = []
    for index, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
        offsets.append(len(pdf))
        pdf += str(index).encode("ascii") + b" 0 obj\n" + body + b"\nendobj\n"

    xref_position = len(pdf)
    size = len(objects) + 1
    pdf += b"xref\n"
    pdf += b"0 " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
    pdf += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
    for offset in offsets:
        pdf += ("%010d 00000 n \n" % offset).encode("ascii")
    pdf += b"trailer\n"
    pdf += b"<< /Size " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b" /Root 1 0 R >>\n"
    pdf += b"startxref\n"
    pdf += str(xref_position).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
    pdf += b"%%EOF\n"
    return bytes(pdf)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Facade coordinating all collaboration sub-stores.

    All public operations are delegated to focused sub-stores that share a
    single re-entrant lock and a single monotonic id generator, giving the
    whole system consistent, thread-safe behaviour.
    """

    def __init__(self, now=None):
        self._now_fn = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._id_counter = 0

        self.users = UserStore(self, self._lock)
        self.projects = ProjectStore(self, self._lock)
        self.tasks = TaskStore(self, self._lock)
        self.messages = MessageStore(self, self._lock)
        self.performance = PerformanceStore(self, self._lock)
        self.reports = ReportStore(self, self._lock)

    # -- shared infrastructure ---------------------------------------------
    def _now(self) -> datetime:
        return self._now_fn()

    def _next_id(self) -> int:
        with self._lock:
            self._id_counter += 1
            return self._id_counter

    # -- user delegation ----------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER):
        return self.users.register_user(username, email, role)

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        return self.users.get_user(user_id)

    def list_users(self):
        return self.users.list_users()

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=None, email=None, role=None):
        return self.users.update_user(
            user_id, username=username, email=email, role=role
        )

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        return self.users.delete_user(user_id)

    # -- project delegation -------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description=""):
        return self.projects.create_project(
            name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description
        )

    def get_project(self, project_id):
        return self.projects.get_project(project_id)

    def list_projects(self):
        return self.projects.list_projects()

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id,
        name=None,
        description=None,
        start_date=None,
        end_date=None,
    ):
        return self.projects.update_project(
            project_id,
            name=name,
            description=description,
            start_date=start_date,
            end_date=end_date,
        )

    def delete_project(self, project_id):
        return self.projects.delete_project(project_id)

    # -- task delegation ----------------------------------------------------
    def create_task(self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None):
        return self.tasks.create_task(project_id, title, assignee_id, deadline)

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        return self.tasks.get_task(task_id)

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id):
        return self.tasks.list_tasks_by_project(project_id)

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id):
        return self.tasks.list_tasks_by_assignee(assignee_id)

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        return self.tasks.assign_task(task_id, assignee_id)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, new_assignee_id):
        return self.tasks.reassign_task(task_id, new_assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id):
        return self.tasks.unassign_task(task_id)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline):
        return self.tasks.set_deadline(task_id, deadline)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status):
        return self.tasks.update_status(task_id, status)

    # -- message delegation -------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id=None,
        task_id=None,
        parent_id=None,
        attachments=None,
    ):
        return self.messages.post_message(
            author_id,
            body,
            project_id=project_id,
            task_id=task_id,
            parent_id=parent_id,
            attachments=attachments,
        )

    def edit_message(self, message_id, author_id, body):
        return self.messages.edit_message(message_id, author_id, body)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, author_id, filename, data):
        return self.messages.add_attachment(message_id, author_id, filename, data)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id):
        return self.messages.list_messages_by_project(project_id)

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id):
        return self.messages.list_messages_by_task(task_id)

    def delete_message(self, message_id, author_id):
        return self.messages.delete_message(message_id, author_id)

    # -- performance delegation ---------------------------------------------
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_completion_rate(user_id)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_average_completion_time(user_id)

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="", task_id=None):
        return self.performance.submit_rating(
            rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback=feedback, task_id=task_id
        )

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_ratings_summary(user_id)

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_user_dashboard(user_id)

    # -- report delegation --------------------------------------------------
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id):
        return self.reports.project_progress_report(project_id)

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None):
        return self.reports.team_performance_report(project_id)

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id):
        return self.reports.individual_performance_report(user_id)

    def export_report(self, report, report_format):
        return self.reports.export_report(report, report_format)


__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",
    "Attachment",
    "User",
    "Project",
    "Task",
    "Message",
    "Rating",
]
